subject: Notes: “BCRS Sales Posting Validation (Finance UAT)” Mar 11, 2026

messages.body:
Notes from “BCRS Sales Posting Validation (Finance UAT)”
These notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.
Open meeting notes
The content was auto-generated on March 11, 2026, 4:12 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.
Summary
Financial reconciliation initiated with sales figure review and BCRS deposit clarification via back office data, culminating with successful SAP posting verification against back office totals.

Sales Figure and BCRS Reconciliation
Participants worked to reconcile sales totals, specifically an 82.19 amount including the BCRS deposit, which did not match SAP figures. They identified that 80.39 was the sum of line items including a service fee and 2 delivery orders.

BCRS and Sales Posting Concerns
Concern was raised that sales posting verification was prioritized over BCRS posting, despite sales posting issues having supposedly been addressed during GST compliance efforts. A simple test order was planned to trace its flow to SAP and verify the sales posting.

SAP Posting Verification Confirmed
The team successfully validated the SAP postings against the DBP totals for 2 test orders, confirming the service fee, shipping charges, and BCRS deposits matched the back office data. All amounts were confirmed as aligning after updating the billing documents.

Suggested next steps
	[Prajney Sribhashyam] Update Script: Add the 2 new orders to the current testing script sheet.
	[Wai Ching Chan] Post Orders: Re-trigger the sales posting for the 2 orders shared in the group chat. Inform the team when posting is complete.
	[Prajney Sribhashyam] Process Documents: Process the recently posted orders further; provide the resulting accounting documents to the team.
	[Prajney Sribhashyam] Request Reposting: Contact the PSR team; request they repost the necessary Scan and Go data.
	[Prajney Sribhashyam] Consolidate Data: Consolidate the data required before passing it to the PSR team for reposting.
	[Onkar Bamane] Repost Data: Repost pre-order and scan and go test data to store 447. Use separate future business dates for each flow without existing transactions.
	[Onkar Bamane] Share Order Info: Provide the invoice number and document number for the new fair price order to Lai Shu Hui.
	[Prajney Sribhashyam] Coordinate Data: Call external party; Ensure data shipment today for readiness by tomorrow.
	[Wai Ching Chan] Group Confirmation: Confirm relevant status details on the communication group; Tag necessary individual.
	[Hendry Tionardi] Share SAP Data: Share SAP entries for the orders posted on the group.
	[Wai Ching Chan] Delete Payload: Delete the shared SML payload.
	[Hendry Tionardi] Update Invoice: Update status with the invoice creation time after approximately 15 minutes.
	[Hendry Tionardi] Paste Screenshot: Paste the comparison screenshot of the back office data onto the group.
	[Wai Ching Chan] Attach Order Number: Add the specific order number to the corresponding message for Kay convenience.
	[Hendry Tionardi] Share BCR Screen: Share the requested BCR screenshot.
	Notes by Gemini

subject: [JIRA] (DPD-715) Dynamic ad slot configuration for Homepage swimlanes

messages.body:
Nikhil Grover made 2 updates

(Ecom/Omni) DPD
 / DPD-715
Dynamic ad slot configuration for Homepage swimlanes

  Nikhil Grover  10:12 PM Singapore Time    
Summary :	Dynamic ad slot configuration for Omni homepage swimlanesDynamic ad slot configuration for Homepage swimlanes

  Nikhil Grover  10:15 PM Singapore Time    
Description :	

As a Product Manager,
I want to control product ad placement and count via a Split feature flag,
So that I can optimize the homepage layout (swimlanes and vertical scroll) on both Omni and OG Homepageswithout code changes or manual API parameter updates.

Acceptance criteria

Scenario: Dynamic API request based on Split configuration

Given the feature flag for swimlanesis ON

And the configuration defines the slots as [3, 5, 7]

When a user navigates to the Omnihomepage

Then the application should request 3 ads for the enabled swimlanes

And render them at positions 3, 5, and 7.

Scenario: Fallback to default

Given the feature flag is OFF

When the Omnihomepage is loaded

Then the application should use the current slots [1,3]

And the API request should fetch 2 ads .for the enabled swimlanes

Scenario: Changing ad density via Split

Given the current Split configuration is [3, 5, 7]

When I update the Split configuration to [2, 4, 6, 8, 10] in the dashboard

And a new user session begins on the Omnihomepage

Then the system should automatically request 5 ads for the enabled swimlanesfrom the API

And the ads should render at the new specified indices without a code deployment.

Scenario: Handling an empty slot configuration

Given the Split configuration is set to an empty array []

When the Omnihomepage is loaded

Then the application should request 0 ads from the Ad API

And the homepage should display only organic products with no ads or gaps.

Scenario: Ad Supply Shortage (Data Integrity)

Given the Split configuration is set to [3, 5, 7] (3 slots)

But the Ad API only returns 2 valid ads

When the homepage renders

Then the system should fill slots 3 and 5

And slot 7 should show organic content.

Scenario 4: Out-of-Bounds Handling

Given the Split configuration includes a slot index of 20

But there are only 10 items available in the swimlaneor in the vertical scroll

When the page renders

Then the system should ignore the index of 20 and render only the ads that fit within the available content range.

Constraint: Integration with Existing Stock Logic

Constraint: The system must continue to honour the existing stock availability check prior to requesting ads. No out of stock products should be shown as ads.

subject: NTUC Enterprise-2025 Web Service Penetration Testing for RMN APIs

messages.body:
Dear Mr. Bui,

Greetings from LGMS.

Thank you for having us on the NTUC Enterprise-2025 Web Service Penetration Testing for RMN APIs initiative. My name is Xiao Cong and I will be the main contact person for this project. 

Kindly find the scope of work of this project tabulated below for your reference. Kindly let me know if there are any discrepancies of the SOW. 

Web Service Penetration Testing (White Box)

Network accessibility: External
Testing Window: During Office Hour
Type of Web Service: Retrieving Banners and Tracking Ad Interactions
Number of API: 1 API
Number of Roles: Not more than 2 roles
Number of Credentials per Role: Not more than 2 user credentials
Number of Functions to be tested in the web service scope: Not more than 3 functions

Please let me know if a kick-off meeting is required on your end, and we will be happy to arrange for one. Meanwhile, kindly find the prerequisites below, as these details are needed to facilitate the PT activities. 

Testing timeline: [1 December 2025 - 5 December 2025? ]
API specification/definition files (postman collection, openAPI, .etc) 
2 credentials (admin role/user role)
API flow documentation (if any)
Confirmation on test environment (UAT/Production)
Permission to Create, Update, Delete Data

Should you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to me (via email or Google Chat - xiaocong.ong_ne@ntucguest.com).

subject: Re: FPG x OSMOS : TTD discussion

messages.body:
Copying @Michael Bui and @Andin Eswarlal Rajesh for technical inputs.

On Mon, Mar 2, 2026 at 10:44 PM Sujit Jha <sujit.jha@osmos.ai> wrote:

Hi Nikhil,

Regarding your question on rendering via the SDK versus using your own components, I wanted to provide some context on why SDK rendering is generally recommended, especially when programmatic demand is involved.

When dealing with programmatic demand, the creative assets come from various external stakeholders. If a buyer sends a "Native" creative, you can deconstruct it into your own UI components. However, if a buyer sends a "Standard Display" creative which is essentially a fixed HTML blob you cannot deconstruct it. In those instances, you would be forced to use the SDK’s renderer to ensure the impression displays correctly. Relying on the SDK for rendering ensures you can support all creative types without technical failures.

Attached herewith is the sample SDK Documentation to evaluate the implementation complexity and resource requirements.

Best,
Sujit J Jha

On Mon, Mar 2, 2026 at 11:48 AM Nikhil Grover <nikhil.grover@fairpricegroup.sg> wrote:

Hi Sujit,

Thanks for the updates. 

A couple of follow-ups:
Implementation complexity also refers to ad format rendering. Can the SDK be used for measurement only or must we also render the ads using the SDK? We would like to retain our existing rendering components and only use the SDK for measurement. 
Do you have an ETA on the SDK's full technical specifications? I need to evaluate the implementation complexity before requesting resources.
Thanks!
Nikhil

On Fri, Feb 27, 2026 at 6:58 PM Sujit Jha <sujit.jha@osmos.ai> wrote:

Hi Nikhil,

Please find the answers to your follow-up questions below:

1. Partner and Tag IDs
TTD will provide these IDs directly for your ads.txt file. Regarding the request flow, even though OSMOS acts as the SSP, TTD's IDs must be listed in the FPG ads.txt file. This allows TTD's advertisers to verify that the publisher has authorized its inventory for TTD. The ads.txt file ensures that your digital ad inventory is only sold through authorized sellers.

2. Ad Sizes
The 2:1 dimension should work for mobile and web via programmatic direct. However, please note that standard IAB sizes typically ensure maximum demand and fill rates from open auctions. While the IAB encourages fixed-ratio banners, industry adoption by marketers is still ongoing. We can specifically test the 2:1 dimensions once API access is granted.

3. Technical Integration
Unfortunately, since TTD does not support a sandbox environment, we are unable to simulate or test the integration without active API access. Using incorrect account IDs also risks the inventory being flagged.

I have also attached the document outlining the major SDK requirements for your review.

Best,

	

Sujit J Jha

Product Management

Can you advise where I can find the partner ID and tag ID? Will TTD provide this?
If the ad request goes from OSMOS to TTD, shouldn't the Ads.txt list OSMOS as well?  
2/ IAB Ad sizes: 

Thanks for sharing the sizes. As I understand it, with the growth of mobile-first environments, IAB New Ad sizes recommend fixed aspect ratio banners for responsive content delivery across different screen sizes rather than fixed dimensions. Sharing the document for reference, please do highlight if I am missing something.

We are currently using a 2:1 dimension banner on the placements where we want to enable programmatic ads. Please let me know if there would be any concern with the dimension.

3/ Technical integration: Understand that OSMOS has already tested programmatic ads with TTD with other retailer partners. Since API access may take time, is there another way we can simulate and derisk the integration? We would like to assure the business team that our setup with the 2:1 banners will deliver programmatic ads via TTD, even if we cannot estimate demand. 

Looking forward to receiving the SDK documentation. 

Warm regards,
Nikhil

On Mon, Feb 23, 2026 at 6:00 PM Sujit Jha <sujit.jha@osmos.ai> wrote:

Hi Nikhil,

Thank you for summarizing our discussion regarding the TTD integration for onsite, offsite, and in-store ads.

Please find the details regarding the allowed/blocked list formats and standard IAB ad sizes below to assist with your evaluation.

1. Format for Allowed and Blocked Lists

In programmatic advertising, lists are typically managed via Ads.txt, App-Ads.txt, and platform-level CSV uploads.

Supply-Side (Ads.txt / App-Ads.txt)
To allow The Trade Desk (Networks) to buy your inventory, they must be listed in your ads.txt file for web or app-ads.txt for mobile apps.

- Format: [Domain of Exchange], [Publisher Account ID], [Account Type], [Authority ID]
- Example for TTD: thetradedesk.com, [Your_Partner_ID], DIRECT, [TAG_ID]

Demand-... [truncated]

Hi Sujit,

As I understand it, the "Standard" creatives come in the fixed sizes standardised by the IAB, which are:

- Smartphone Banner (320 x 50 / 300 x 50): Standard footer/header units.
- Interstitials (320 x 480 for Phone / 768 x 1024 for Tablet): Full-screen units between actions.
- MREC (300 x 250): Integrated directly into feeds.

Since our banner carousel is 2:1 and we do not foresee changing this dimension, I believe we should stick to "Native" formats. These will also provide a better customer experience because they look like part of the app's content. 

Accordingly, we should be able to use our existing banner component for rendering and the OSMOS SDK for tracking. 

Let me know your thoughts on the feasibility and whether this might adversely impact demand generation.

Thanks!
Nikhil

subject: GCP Service Account Key clean UP

messages.body:
Dear All,

As you are aware, we have automated service account key rotation in place for GCP. However, this process does not include many legacy service accounts. I've attached a spreadsheet listing these SAs, which either have keys that were never rotated or have more than two keys.

Please review the list and indicate your consent to include these SAs in the key rotation process by filling in the relevant information within the spreadsheet.

Thank you for your time on this matter.

GCP SA with Key

Regards,
Mohit Niranwal

Hi Jazz

Thank you for your support so far in helping us address the service account key concern. I would like to draw your attention to the service account key decommissioning activity for the DPD projects.

Based on our latest review, we have identified several service accounts that are still holding legacy service account keys. As part of our security best practices, these outdated keys need to be removed from the GCP projects. A similar practice is currently being implemented across other projects as well to ensure consistent security standards.

We have gathered the relevant information and attached a spreadsheet listing the service accounts that either have keys that were never rotated or currently have more than two active keys. This information is provided to obtain confirmation on including these service accounts in our automated key rotation process.

To proceed with the clean-up and decommissioning activity, could you please let us know the appropriate contact person whom we may coordinate with for this task? We are planning to schedule a discussion on Friday, 13th March, to review the remaining items and agree on the next steps.

Your support in helping us complete this security improvement initiative would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Regards
Himal Hewagamage

On Tue, Mar 10, 2026 at 3:58 PM Himal Hewagamage <himal.hewagamage@fairpricegroup.sg> wrote:

Hi Jazz

Thank you for your support so far in helping us address the service account key concern. I would like to draw your attention to the service account key decommissioning activity for the DPD projects.

Based on our latest review, we have identified several service accounts that are still holding legacy service account keys. As part of our security best practices, these outdated keys need to be removed from the GCP projects. A similar practice is currently being implemented across other projects as well to ensure consistent security standards.

We have gathered the relevant information and attached a spreadsheet listing the service accounts that either have keys that were never rotated or currently have more than two active keys. This information is provided to obtain confirmation on including these service accounts in our automated key rotation process.

To proceed with the clean-up and decommissioning activity, could you please let us know the appropriate contact person whom we may coordinate with for this task? We are planning to schedule a discussion on Friday, 13th March, to review the remaining items and agree on the next steps.

Your support in helping us complete this security improvement initiative would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Regards
Himal Hewagamage

Please review the list and indicate your consent to include these SAs in the key rotation process by filling in the relevant information within the spreadsheet.

Thank you for your time on this matter.
